key_insight: Preference Pareto Exploration (PPE) lets users navigate deep multi-task trade-offs interactively by moving along the Pareto manifold while respecting chosen preferences.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical way to tune deep multi-task models when simple weighted-sum losses are too restrictive or too expensive to retune repeatedly.
method: Predictor-corrector optimization on the Pareto-optimal manifold
Predictor step follows the decision maker’s preference tangentially
Krylov subspace approximation avoids explicit Hessian computation using matrix-vector products via automatic differentiation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation on toy problems and deep learning examples, showing functionality and performance in both settings.
watch_item: Interactive Pareto navigation may still depend on a well-behaved Pareto geometry and careful numerical tuning of the predictor-corrector and Krylov steps.
paper_title: Interactive Pareto navigation for deep multi-task learning
